title: OS commands should not be vulnerable to argument injection attacks
description: OS command argument injections occur when applications allow the execution of operating system commands from untrusted data but the untrusted data is limited to the arguments. It is not possible to directly inject arbitrary commands that compromise the underlying operating system, but the behavior of the executed command still might be influenced in a way that allows to expand access, for example, execution of arbitrary commands. The security of the application depends on the behavior of the application that is executed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: String concatenation in exec() calls is vulnerable to injection attacks
Template literals in exec() calls are vulnerable to injection attacks
String concatenation in execSync() calls is vulnerable to injection attacks
Dynamic command construction in spawn() is vulnerable to injection attacks
